Hello Manav Shah — nice to meet you!

How can I help today? Here are a few things I can do:
- Answer questions or explain concepts
- Help with writing, editing, or resumes
- Assist with coding, debugging, or algorithms
- Do math or data analysis
- Brainstorm ideas or plan projects
- Provide study help or create summaries

Would you like me to call you "Manav," or do you prefer a different name or pronouns?

runs.content:
I don’t have access to your system or chat infrastructure, so I can’t see or provide any session ID for you.

If you meant a session ID in a specific environment, here are ways to find it depending on context — tell me which one you mean and I can give more precise steps.

- Browser (web app)
  - Chrome DevTools: Application > Storage > Cookies — look for session cookie (common names: session, sessionid, JSESSIONID, PHPSESSID).
  - Console: view all cookies
    ```
    document.cookie
    ```
    or get a cookie by name:
    ```js
    function getCookie(name){
      return document.cookie.split('; ').find(row => row.startsWith(name + '='))?.split('=')[1]
    }
    ```
- HTTP request/response
  - Inspect Network tab in DevTools and look at request headers (Authorization, Cookie) or response Set-Cookie header.
  - Example curl showing headers:
    ```
    curl -I https://example.com
    ```
- Server-side frameworks
  - Express (Node.js): session ID is often available as `req.sessionID` (if using express-session).
  - PHP: `session_id()` returns the current session id.
  - Django: `request.session.session_key`
  - Rails: session data is typically in cookies — check `cookies.encrypted[:_yourapp_session]` or server logs.
- CLI / environment variables
  - Some systems store tokens in env vars: `echo $SESSION_ID` (or the specific env var name).
- If you’re debugging an API client
  - Log the `Authorization` or custom header your client sends, or dump the token before sending.

If you tell me the platform (browser, Node/Express, Django, PHP, mobile app, specific website), I’ll give exact commands or code to retrieve the session id.
